summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Dark_Net MLBB clan website. Backend: 19/19 pytest cases passed (100%). Frontend: all critical user flows verified end-to-end via Playwright (Home/About/Members/Join Us/Login/Admin/Logout). Application submission, admin login, admin dashboard, and announcement creation all working. No critical issues found.

minor.issue: CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True. Browsers ignore credentialed CORS with wildcard. Works here because cookies are same-origin via ingress, but should set explicit allowed origin list.
Accepts `status` as query param (not request body). Functional but unconventional; consider PATCH with JSON body.
POST is unauthenticated (intentional for public form) and not rate-limited — vulnerable to spam. Add basic rate limiting / captcha for production.
Cookies set with secure=False. Acceptable for preview but must be True in production HTTPS.

issues: Cyberpunk theme is on-brand; neon blue/purple/red accents render well. No contrast issues observed in tested viewports.

action_items: Replace SendGrid placeholder API key with a real one before launch (currently emails silently fail; DB record is still created — verified).
Tighten CORS: replace allow_origins=['*'] with explicit production origins since allow_credentials=True is set.
Set cookie secure=True for production HTTPS deployment.
Consider rate limiting on POST /api/applications and POST /api/auth/login to prevent spam/brute-force.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 491-line file mixing auth, models, routes, email, and seeding — should be split into auth.py / models.py / routes_*.py modules for maintainability.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook recommends 5-fail lockout). Not currently implemented.
JWT_SECRET is loaded but no fallback validation — server will crash on startup if missing (acceptable fail-fast).
Refresh token is issued but no /api/auth/refresh endpoint exists to actually use it.
PATCH /api/applications/{email}/status: using email as path key allows injection of arbitrary email casing/values; consider using application _id instead.
All admin endpoints correctly enforce role=='admin' via get_admin_user — security boundary verified.

success_rate.frontend: 100% functional (all critical flows verified end-to-end; 3 query_selector checks in script had timing races but the subsequent functional steps confirmed those screens loaded correctly)

seed_data_creation: Backend already seeds 2 members (DarkNet_Shadow, DarkNet_Phantom) and 2 announcements (Welcome to Dark_Net, Weekend Tournament) on startup. Verified present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_darknet_api.py covers all 19 critical endpoints (public reads, auth login/register/logout/me, applications submit + admin moderation, announcements CRUD, members CRUD). Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_darknet_api.py -v. Frontend uses data-testid throughout (join-darknet-btn, application-form-title, login-title, admin-dashboard-title, member-card-{game_name}, tab-applications/members/announcements, etc.). SENDGRID is MOCKED via placeholder key — email send fails silently but DB persists application (verified). Admin seed is idempotent and updates password if changed (auth playbook compliant).
